metadata.summary: Sage: Open Source Mathematics Software: Build system of the Sage documentation

documentation:
================================================================================
 Sage: Open Source Mathematics Software: Build system of the Sage documentation
================================================================================

About SageMath
--------------

   "Creating a Viable Open Source Alternative to
    Magma, Maple, Mathematica, and MATLAB"

   Copyright (C) 2005-2020 The Sage Development Team

   https://www.sagemath.org

SageMath fully supports all major Linux distributions, recent versions of macOS, and Windows (using Windows Subsystem for Linux).

The traditional and recommended way to install SageMath is from source via Sage-the-distribution (https://www.sagemath.org/download-source.html).  Sage-the-distribution first builds a large number of open source packages from source (unless it finds suitable versions installed in the system) and then installs the Sage Library (sagelib, implemented in Python and Cython).


About this pip-installable source distribution
----------------------------------------------

This is the build system of the Sage documentation, based on Sphinx.

modules.name: sage_docbuild.vendor
documentation: "sage -python -m sage_docbuild.vendor" updates src/doc/common/_vendor/*.inv

modules.name: sage_docbuild.utils
documentation: Utilities

functions.name: sage_docbuild.utils.build_many
documentation:
Map a list of arguments in `args` to a single-argument target function
`target` in parallel using `multiprocessing.cpu_count()` (or
`processes` if given) simultaneous processes.

This is a simplified version of `multiprocessing.Pool.map` from the
Python standard library which avoids a couple of its pitfalls. In
particular, it can abort (with a {class}`RuntimeError`)
without hanging if one of
the worker processes unexpectedly dies. It also has semantics equivalent
to `maxtasksperchild=1`; that is, one process is started per argument.
As such, this is inefficient for processing large numbers of fast tasks,
but appropriate for running longer tasks (such as doc builds) which may
also require significant cleanup.

It also avoids starting new processes from a pthread, which results in at
least one known issue:

- When PARI is built with multi-threading support, forking a Sage
  process from a thread leaves the main Pari interface instance broken
  (see {issue}`26608#comment:38`).

In the future this may be replaced by a generalized version of the more
robust parallel processing implementation from `sage.doctest.forker`.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.utils import build_many
sage: def target(N):
....:     import time
....:     time.sleep(float(0.1))
....:     print('Processed task %s' % N)
sage: _ = build_many(target, range(8), processes=8)
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
Processed task ...
```

This version can also return a result, and thus can
be used as a replacement for `multiprocessing.Pool.map` (i.e. it still
blocks until the result is ready):

```
sage: def square(N):
....:     return N * N
sage: build_many(square, range(100))
[0, 1, 4, 9, ..., 9604, 9801]
```

If the target function raises an exception in any of the workers,
`build_many` raises that exception and all other results are discarded.
Any in-progress tasks may still be allowed to complete gracefully before
the exception is raised:

```
sage: def target(N):
....:     import time, os, signal
....:     if N == 4:
....:         # Task 4 is a poison pill
....:         1 / 0
....:     else:
....:         time.sleep(float(0.5))
....:         print('Processed task %s' % N)
```

Note: In practice this test might still show output from the other worker
processes before the poison-pill is executed. It may also display the
traceback from the failing process on stderr. However, due to how the
doctest runner works, the doctest will only expect the final exception:

```
sage: build_many(target, range(8), processes=8)
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
    raise ZeroDivisionError("rational division by zero")
 ZeroDivisionError: rational division by zero
...
    raise worker_exc.original_exception
ZeroDivisionError: rational division by zero
```

Similarly, if one of the worker processes dies unexpectedly otherwise exits
non-zero (e.g. killed by a signal) any in-progress tasks will be completed
gracefully, but then a {class}`RuntimeError` is raised and pending tasks
are not started:

```
sage: def target(N):
....:     import time, os, signal
....:     if N == 4:
....:         # Task 4 is a poison pill
....:         os.kill(os.getpid(), signal.SIGKILL)
....:     else:
....:         time.sleep(float(0.5))
....:         print('Processed task %s' % N)
sage: build_many(target, range(8), processes=8)
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
WorkerDiedException: worker for 4 died with non-zero exit code -9
```

modules.name: sage_docbuild.__main__
documentation:
Sage docbuild main

This module defines the Sage documentation build command:

```
sage --docbuild [OPTIONS] DOCUMENT (FORMAT | COMMAND)
```

If `FORMAT` is given, it builds `DOCUMENT` in `FORMAT`. If `COMMAND` is
given, it returns information about `DOCUMENT`.

Run `sage --docbuild` to get detailed explanations about
arguments and options.

Positional arguments:

```
DOCUMENT              name of the document to build. It can be either one of
                      the documents listed by -D or 'file=/path/to/FILE' to
                      build documentation for this specific file.
FORMAT or COMMAND     document output format (or command)
```

Standard options:

```
-h, --help            show a help message and exit
-H, --help-all        show an extended help message and exit
-D, --documents       list all available DOCUMENTs
-F, --formats         list all output FORMATs
-C DOC, --commands DOC
                      list all COMMANDs for DOCUMENT DOC; use 'all' to list all
-i, --inherited       include inherited members in reference manual; may be
                      slow, may fail for PDF output
-u, --underscore      include variables prefixed with '_' in reference
                      manual; may be slow, may fail for PDF output
-j, --mathjax, --jsmath
                      ignored for backwards compatibility
--no-plot             do not include graphics auto-generated using the '.. plot' markup
--no-preparsed-examples
                      do not show preparsed versions of EXAMPLES blocks
--include-tests-blocks
                      include TESTS blocks in the reference manual
--no-pdf-links        do not include PDF links in DOCUMENT 'website';
                      FORMATs: html, json, pickle, web
--live-doc            make Sage code blocks live for html FORMAT
--warn-links          issue a warning whenever a link is not properly
                      resolved; equivalent to '--sphinx-opts -n' (sphinx
                      option: nitpicky)
--check-nested        check picklability of nested classes in DOCUMENT 'reference'
--no-prune-empty-dirs
                      do not prune empty directories in the documentation sources
--use-cdns            assume internet connection and use CDNs; in particular,
                      use MathJax CDN
-N, --no-colors       do not color output; does not affect children
-q, --quiet           work quietly; same as --verbose=0
-v LEVEL, --verbose LEVEL
                      report progress at LEVEL=0 (quiet), 1 (normal), 2
                      (info), or 3 (debug); does not affect children
-o DIR, --output DIR  if DOCUMENT is a single file ('file=...'), write output
                      to this directory
```

Advanced options:

```
Use these options with care.

-S OPTS, --sphinx-opts OPTS
                      pass comma-separated OPTS to sphinx-build; must precede
                      OPTS with '=', as in '-S=-q,-aE' or '-S="-q,-aE"'
-U, --update-mtimes   before building reference manual, update modification
                      times for auto-generated reST files
-k, --keep-going      Do not abort on errors but continue as much as possible
                      after an error
--all-documents ARG   if ARG is 'reference', list all subdocuments of
                      en/reference. If ARG is 'all', list all main documents
```
sage.doctest: needs sphinx

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.format_columns
documentation:
Utility function that formats a list as a simple table and returns
a Unicode string representation.

The number of columns is
computed from the other options, unless it's passed as a keyword
argument.  For help on Python's string formatter, see

https://docs.python.org/library/string.html#format-string-syntax

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_usage
documentation:
Append and return a brief usage message for the Sage documentation builder.

If 'compact' is False, the function adds a final newline character.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_description
documentation:
Append and return a brief description of the Sage documentation builder.

If 'compact' is `False`, the function adds a final newline character.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_examples
documentation: Append and return some usage examples for the Sage documentation builder.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_documents
documentation:
Append and return a tabular list of documents, including a
shortcut 'all' for all documents, available to the Sage
documentation builder.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.get_formats
documentation:
Return a list of output formats the Sage documentation builder
will accept on the command-line.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_formats
documentation:
Append and return a tabular list of output formats available to
the Sage documentation builder.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_commands
documentation:
Append and return a tabular list of commands, if any, the Sage
documentation builder can run on the indicated document.  The
default is to list all commands for all documents.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.setup_parser
documentation:
Set up and return a command-line ArgumentParser instance for the
Sage documentation builder.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.setup_logger
documentation:
Set up a Python Logger instance for the Sage documentation builder.

The optional argument sets logger's level and message format.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.__main__ import setup_logger, logger
sage: setup_logger()
sage: type(logger)
<class 'logging.Logger'>
```

classes.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_message_long
documentation:
Print an extended help message for the Sage documentation builder
and exits.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_message_short
documentation:
Print a help message for the Sage documentation builder.

The message includes command-line usage and a list of options.
The message is printed only on the first call.  If error is True
during this call, the message is printed only if the user hasn't
requested a list (e.g., documents, formats, commands).

classes.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.help_wrapper
documentation:
A helper wrapper for command-line options to the Sage
documentation builder that print lists, such as document names,
formats, and document-specific commands.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.__main__.IntersphinxCache

documentation:
Return the result of `sphinx.ext.intersphinx.fetch_inventory()`
from a cache if possible. Otherwise, call
`sphinx.ext.intersphinx.fetch_inventory()` and cache the result.

Replace sphinx.ext.intersphinx.fetch_inventory by an in-memory

This file contains configuration needed to customize Sphinx input and output

functions.name: sage_docbuild.conf.call_intersphinx
documentation:
Call intersphinx and make links between Sage manuals relative. TESTS: Check that the link from the thematic tutorials to the reference manual is relative, see {issue}`20118`: ``` sage: from sage.env import SAGE_DOC sage: thematic_index = os.path.join(SAGE_DOC, "html", "en", "thematic_tutorials", "index.html") sage: for line in open(thematic_index).readlines(): # optional - sagemath_doc_html ....: if "padics" in line: ....: _ = sys.stdout.write(line)

  • Introduction to the p-adics

    • ```

functions.name: sage_docbuild.conf.find_sage_dangling_links
documentation: Try to find dangling link in local module imports or all.py.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.conf.check_nested_class_picklability
documentation: Print a warning if pickling is broken for nested classes.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.conf.skip_member
documentation:
To suppress Sphinx warnings / errors, we

- Don't include [aliases of] builtins.
- Don't include the docstring for any nested class which has been
  inserted into its module by
  {class}`sage.misc.NestedClassMetaclass` only for pickling. The
  class will be properly documented inside its surrounding class.
- Optionally, check whether pickling is broken for nested classes.
- Optionally, include objects whose name begins with an underscore
  ('\_'), i.e., "private" or "hidden" attributes, methods, etc.

Otherwise, we abide by Sphinx's decision. Note: The object
`obj` is excluded (included) if this handler returns True
(False).

functions.name: sage_docbuild.conf.setup
documentation: This replaces the setup() in sage.misc.sagedoc_conf

variables.name: sage_docbuild.conf.copybutton_exclude
documentation: exclude single comments (in particular, # optional!)

classes.name: sage_docbuild.conf.SagecodeTransform
documentation:
Transform a code block to a live code block enabled by jupyter-sphinx.

Effectively a code block like:

```
EXAMPLE::

    sage: 1 + 1
    2
```

is transformed into:

```
EXAMPLE::

    sage: 1 + 1
    2

.. ONLY:: html

    .. JUPYTER-EXECUTE::
        :hide-code:
        :hide-output:
        :raises:
        :stderr:

        1 + 1
```

enabling live execution of the code.

modules.name: sage_docbuild.ext.inventory_builder
documentation:
Inventory builder

A customized builder which only generates intersphinx "object.inv"
inventory files. The documentation files are not written.

A customized builder which only generates intersphinx "object.inv"

modules.name: sage_docbuild.build_options
documentation:
Build options

This module defines options for building Sage documentation.

variables.name: sage_docbuild.build_options.OMIT
documentation: docs/dirs to omit when listing and building 'all'

modules.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs
documentation:
Sage multidocs extension

The goal of this extension is to manage a multi-documentation in Sphinx. To be
able to compile Sage's huge documentation in parallel, the documentation is cut
into a bunch of independent documentations called "sub-docs", which are
compiled separately. There is a master document which points to all the
sub-docs. The intersphinx extension ensures that the cross-link between the
sub-docs are correctly resolved. However some work is needed to build a global
index. This is the goal of the `multidocs` extension.

More precisely this extension ensures the correct merging of

- the todo list if this extension is activated
- the python indexes
- the list of python modules
- the javascript index
- the citations
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.merge_environment
documentation:
Merge the following attributes of the sub-docs environment into the main
environment:

- `titles` -- Titles
- `todo_all_todos` -- todo's
- `indexentries` -- global python index
- `all_docs` -- needed by the js index
- `citations` -- citations
- `domaindata['py']['modules']` -- list of python modules

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.get_env
documentation: Get the environment of a sub-doc from the pickle

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.merge_js_index
documentation: Merge the JS indexes of the sub-docs into the main JS index

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.get_js_index
documentation: Get the JS index of a sub-doc from the file

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.fix_path_html
documentation:
Fix the context so that the files

- {file}`search.html`
- {file}`genindex.html`
- {file}`py-modindex.html`

point to the right place, that is in {file}`reference/` instead of
{file}`reference/subdocument`.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.write_citations
documentation: Pickle the citation in a file.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.fetch_citation
documentation:
Fetch the global citation index from the refman to allow for cross
references.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.multidocs.init_subdoc
documentation:
Init the merger depending on if we are compiling a sub-doc or the master
doc itself.

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: sage_docbuild.builders
documentation:
Documentation builders

This module is the starting point for building documentation, and is
responsible to figure out what to build and with which options. The actual
documentation build for each individual document is then done in a subprocess
call to Sphinx, see {func}`builder_helper`. Note that

- The builders are configured with `build_options.py`;
- The Sphinx subprocesses are configured in `conf.py`.

{class}`DocBuilder` is the base class of all Builders. It has builder helpers
{meth}`html()`, {meth}`latex`, {meth}`pdf`, {meth}`inventory`, etc, which are
invoked depending on the output type. Each type corresponds with the Sphinx
builder format, except that `pdf` is Sphinx latex builder plus compiling
latex to pdf. Note that Sphinx inventory builder is not native to Sphinx
but provided by Sage. See `sage_docbuild/ext/inventory_builder.py`. The
Sphinx inventory builder is a dummy builder with no actual output but produces
doctree files in `local/share/doctree` and `inventory.inv` inventory files
in `local/share/inventory`.

The reference manual is built in two passes, first by {class}`ReferenceBuilder`
with `inventory` output type and secondly with\`\`html\`\` output type. The
{class}`ReferenceBuilder` itself uses {class}`ReferenceTopBuilder` and
{class}`ReferenceSubBuilder` to build subcomponents of the reference manual.
The {class}`ReferenceSubBuilder` examines the modules included in the
subcomponent by comparing the modification times of the module files with the
times saved in `local/share/doctree/reference.pickle` from the previous
build. Then new rst files are generated for new and updated modules. See
{meth}`get_new_and_updated_modules()`.

After {issue}`31948`, when Sage is built, {class}`ReferenceBuilder` is not used
and its responsibility is now taken by the `Makefile` in `$SAGE_ROOT/src/doc`.
sage.doctest: needs sphinx

functions.name: sage_docbuild.builders.build_ref_doc
documentation: Parallel Building Ref Manual      #

functions.name: sage_docbuild.builders.builder_helper
documentation:
Return a function which builds the documentation for
output type `type`.

TESTS:

Check that {issue}`25161` has been resolved:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import DocBuilder
sage: from sage_docbuild.__main__ import setup_parser
sage: DocBuilder._options = setup_parser().parse_args([]) # builder_helper needs _options to be set

sage: import sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild
sage: def raiseBaseException():
....:     raise BaseException("abort pool operation")
sage: original_runsphinx, sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.runsphinx = sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.runsphinx, raiseBaseException

sage: from sage.misc.temporary_file import tmp_dir
sage: os.environ['SAGE_DOC'] = tmp_dir()
sage: sage.env.var('SAGE_DOC') # random
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import builder_helper, build_ref_doc
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import _build_many as build_many
sage: helper = builder_helper("html")
sage: try:  # optional - sagemath_doc_html
....:     build_many(build_ref_doc, [("docname", "en", "html", {})])
....: except Exception as E:
....:     "Non-exception during docbuild: abort pool operation" in str(E)
True
```
Builders                   #

functions.name: sage_docbuild.builders.build_many
documentation:
Thin wrapper around `sage_docbuild.utils.build_many` which uses the
docbuild settings `NUM_THREADS` and `ABORT_ON_ERROR`.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.builders.build_other_doc
documentation: Parallel Building Ref Manual      #

functions.name: sage_docbuild.builders.get_builder
documentation:
Return an appropriate *Builder* object for the document `name`.

DocBuilder and its subclasses do all the real work in building the
documentation.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.builders.get_documents
documentation:
Return a list of document names the Sage documentation builder
will accept as command-line arguments.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.DocBuilder.__init__
documentation:
INPUT:

- `name` -- the name of a subdirectory in SAGE_DOC_SRC, such as
  'tutorial' or 'bordeaux_2008'
- `lang` -- (default "en") the language of the document.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.DocBuilder._output_dir
documentation:
Return the directory where the output of type `type` is stored.

If the directory does not exist, then it will automatically be
created.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import DocBuilder
sage: b = DocBuilder('tutorial')
sage: b._output_dir('html')         # optional - sagemath_doc_html
'.../html/en/tutorial'
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.DocBuilder._doctrees_dir
documentation:
Return the directory where the doctrees are stored.

If the directory does not exist, then it will automatically be
created.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import DocBuilder
sage: b = DocBuilder('tutorial')
sage: b._doctrees_dir()             # optional - sagemath_doc_html
'.../doctrees/en/tutorial'
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.DocBuilder._output_formats
documentation:
Return a list of the possible output formats.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import DocBuilder
sage: b = DocBuilder('tutorial')
sage: b._output_formats()
['changes', 'html', 'htmlhelp', 'inventory', 'json', 'latex', 'linkcheck', 'pickle', 'web']
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.DocBuilder.pdf
documentation:
Build the PDF files for this document.

This is done by first (re)-building the LaTeX output, going
into that LaTeX directory, and running 'make all-pdf' there.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import DocBuilder
sage: b = DocBuilder('tutorial')
sage: b.pdf() #not tested
```

classes.name: sage_docbuild.builders.AllBuilder

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.AllBuilder.__getattr__
documentation:
For any attributes not explicitly defined, we just go through
all of the documents and call their attr.  For example,
'AllBuilder().json()' will go through all of the documents
and call the json() method on their builders.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.AllBuilder._wrapper
documentation:
This is the function which goes through all of the documents
and does the actual building.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.AllBuilder.get_all_documents
documentation:
Return a list of all of the documents.

A document is a directory within one of the language
subdirectories of SAGE_DOC_SRC specified by the global
LANGUAGES variable.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import AllBuilder
sage: documents = AllBuilder().get_all_documents()
sage: 'en/tutorial' in documents  # optional - sage_spkg
True
sage: documents[0] == 'en/reference'
True
```
A class used to build all of the documentation.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.WebsiteBuilder.html
documentation:
After we have finished building the website index page, we copy
everything one directory up, that is, to the base diectory `html/en`.

In addition, an index file is installed into the root doc directory.

Thus we have three index.html files:

> html/en/website/index.html (not used)
> html/en/index.html (base directory)
> index.html (root doc directory)

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.WebsiteBuilder.pdf
documentation: Build the website hosting pdf docs.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.WebsiteBuilder.clean
documentation:
When we clean the output for the website index, we need to
remove all of the HTML that were placed in the parent
directory.

In addition, remove the index file installed into the root doc directory.

Return the directory where the output of type `type` is stored.

If the directory does not exist, then it will automatically be
created.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import ReferenceBuilder
sage: b = ReferenceBuilder('reference')
sage: b._output_dir('html')         # optional - sagemath_doc_html
'.../html/en/reference'
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceBuilder._build_bibliography

Build the bibliography only

The bibliography references.aux is referenced by the other
manuals and needs to be built first.

documentation: Build the entire reference manual except the bibliography

Build reference manuals: build the
top-level document and its components.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceBuilder.get_all_documents
documentation:
Return a list of all reference manual components to build.

We add a component name if it's a subdirectory of the manual's
directory and contains a file named 'index.rst'.

We return the largest component (most subdirectory entries)
first since they will take the longest to build.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import ReferenceBuilder
sage: b = ReferenceBuilder('reference')
sage: refdir = os.path.join(os.environ['SAGE_DOC_SRC'], 'en', b.name)  # optional - sage_spkg
sage: sorted(b.get_all_documents(refdir))  # optional - sage_spkg
['reference/algebras',
 'reference/arithgroup',
 ...,
 'reference/valuations']
```

This class builds the reference manual.  It uses DocBuilder to
build the top-level page and ReferenceSubBuilder for each
sub-component.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceTopBuilder

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceTopBuilder._output_dir
documentation:
Return the directory where the output of type `type` is stored.

If the directory does not exist, then it will automatically be
created.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import ReferenceTopBuilder
sage: b = ReferenceTopBuilder('reference')
sage: b._output_dir('html')         # optional - sagemath_doc_html
'.../html/en/reference'
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceTopBuilder.html
documentation: Build the top-level document.
This class builds the top-level page of the reference manual.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder._wrapper
documentation:
This is the wrapper around the builder_helper methods that
goes through and makes sure things are up to date.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.cache_filename
documentation:
Return the filename where the pickle of the reference cache
is stored.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_cache
documentation:
Retrieve the reference cache which contains the options previously used
by the reference builder.

If it doesn't exist, then we just return an empty dictionary.  If it
is corrupted, return an empty dictionary.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.save_cache
documentation: Pickle the current reference cache for later retrieval.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_sphinx_environment
documentation: Return the Sphinx environment for this project.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.update_mtimes
documentation:
Update the modification times for reST files in the Sphinx
environment for this project.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_modified_modules
documentation:
Return an iterator for all the modules that have been modified
since the documentation was last built.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.print_modified_modules
documentation:
Print a list of all the modules that have been modified since
the documentation was last built.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_all_rst_files
documentation:
Return an iterator for all rst files which are not
autogenerated.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_all_included_modules
documentation:
Return an iterator for all modules which are included in the
reference manual.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_new_and_updated_modules
documentation:
Return an iterator for all new and updated modules that appear in
the toctrees, and remove obsolete old modules.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.print_new_and_updated_modules
documentation:
Print all the modules that appear in the toctrees that
are newly included or updated.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_modules
documentation:
Given a filename for a reST file, return an iterator for
all of the autogenerated reST files that it includes.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_module_docstring_title
documentation: Return the title of the module from its docstring.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.auto_rest_filename
documentation:
Return the name of the file associated to a given module

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sage_docbuild.builders import ReferenceSubBuilder
sage: ReferenceSubBuilder("reference").auto_rest_filename("sage.combinat.partition")
'.../en/reference/sage/combinat/partition.rst'
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.write_auto_rest_file
documentation: Write the autogenerated reST file for module_name.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.clean_auto
documentation: Remove all autogenerated reST files.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.get_unincluded_modules
documentation:
Return an iterator for all the modules in the Sage library
which are not included in the reference manual.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.print_unincluded_modules
documentation:
Print all of the modules which are not included in the Sage
reference manual.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.ReferenceSubBuilder.print_included_modules
documentation:
Print all of the modules that are included in the Sage reference
manual.

This class builds sub-components of the reference manual. It is
responsible for making sure that the auto generated reST files for the
Sage library are up to date.

When building any output, we must first go through and check
to see if we need to update any of the autogenerated reST
files.  There are two cases where this would happen:

1. A new module gets added to one of the toctrees.

2. The actual module gets updated and possibly contains a new
   title.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.builders.SingleFileBuilder

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.SingleFileBuilder.__init__
documentation:
INPUT:

- `path` -- the path to the file for which documentation
  should be built

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.SingleFileBuilder._output_dir
documentation:
Return the directory where the output of type `type` is stored.

If the directory does not exist, then it will automatically be
created.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.builders.SingleFileBuilder._doctrees_dir
documentation:
Return the directory where the doctrees are stored.

If the directory does not exist, then it will automatically be
created.

This is the class used to build the documentation for a single
user-specified file. If the file is called 'foo.py', then the
documentation is built in `DIR/foo/` if the user passes the
command line option "-o DIR", or in `DOT_SAGE/docbuild/foo/`
otherwise.

modules.name: sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild
documentation:
Sphinx build script

This is Sage's version of the `sphinx-build` script. We redirect `stdout` and
`stderr` to our own logger, and remove some unwanted chatter.
sage.doctest: needs sphinx

functions.name: sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.term_width_line
documentation: override the fancy multi-line formatting

classes.name: sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.SageSphinxLogger

methods.name: sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.SageSphinxLogger._check_errors
documentation:
Search for errors in line.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sys import stdout
sage: from sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild import SageSphinxLogger
sage: logger = SageSphinxLogger(stdout, "doctesting")
sage: logger._log_line("Segmentation fault!\n") # indirect doctest
[doctestin] Segmentation fault!
sage: logger.raise_errors()
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
OSError: Segmentation fault!
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.SageSphinxLogger._log_line
documentation:
Write `line` to the output stream with some mangling.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sys import stdout
sage: from sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild import SageSphinxLogger
sage: logger = SageSphinxLogger(stdout, "doctesting")
sage: logger._log_line("building documentation…\n")
[doctestin] building documentation…
```

TESTS:

Verify that {issue}`25160` has been resolved:

```
sage: logger = SageSphinxLogger(stdout, "#25160")
sage: import traceback
sage: try:
....:     raise Exception("artificial exception")
....: except Exception:
....:     for line in traceback.format_exc().split('\n'):
....:         logger._log_line(line)
[#25160   ] Traceback (most recent call last):
[#25160   ]   File ...
[#25160   ]     raise Exception("artificial exception")
[#25160   ] Exception: artificial exception
```

methods.name: sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild.SageSphinxLogger.raise_errors
documentation:
Raise an exceptions if any errors have been found while parsing the
Sphinx output.

EXAMPLES:

```
sage: from sys import stdout
sage: from sage_docbuild.sphinxbuild import SageSphinxLogger
sage: logger = SageSphinxLogger(stdout, "doctesting")
sage: logger._log_line("This is a SEVERE error\n")
[doctestin] This is a SEVERE error
sage: logger.raise_errors()
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
OSError: This is a SEVERE error
```

This implements the file object interface to serve as
`sys.stdout`/`sys.stderr` replacement.

modules.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc
documentation:
Sage autodoc extension

This is {mod}`sphinx.ext.autodoc` extension modified for Sage objects.

The original header of {mod}`sphinx.ext.autodoc`:

> Extension to create automatic documentation from code docstrings.
>
> Automatically insert docstrings for functions, classes or whole modules into
> the doctree, thus avoiding duplication between docstrings and documentation
> for those who like elaborate docstrings.

This module is currently based on {mod}`sphinx.ext.autodoc` from Sphinx version
7.2.6. Compare (do diff) with the upstream source file
[sphinx/ext/autodoc/\_\_init\_\_.py](https://github.com/sphinx-doc/sphinx/blob/v7.2.6/sphinx/ext/autodoc/__init__.py).

In the source file of this module, major modifications are delimited by a pair
of comment dividers. To lessen maintenance burden, we aim at reducing the modifications.

AUTHORS:

- ? (before 2011): initial version derived from sphinx.ext.autodoc
- Johan S. R. Nielsen: support for \_sage_argspec\_
- Simon King (2011-04): use sageinspect; include public cython attributes
  only in the documentation if they have a docstring
- Kwankyu Lee (2018-12-26, 2022-11-08): rebased on the latest sphinx.ext.autodoc
- Kwankyu Lee (2024-02-14): rebased on Sphinx 7.2.6

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.members_option
documentation: Used to convert the :members: option to auto directives.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.exclude_members_option
documentation: Used to convert the :exclude-members: option.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.inherited_members_option
documentation: Used to convert the :inherited-members: option to auto directives.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.member_order_option
documentation: Used to convert the :member-order: option to auto directives.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.class_doc_from_option
documentation: Used to convert the :class-doc-from: option to autoclass directives.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.bool_option
documentation:
Used to convert flag options to auto directives.  (Instead of
directives.flag(), which returns None).

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.merge_members_option
documentation:
Merge :private-members: and :special-members: options to the
:members: option.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.cut_lines
documentation:
Return a listener that removes the first *pre* and last *post*
lines of every docstring. If *what* is a sequence of strings,
only docstrings of a type in *what* will be processed.

Use like this (e.g. in the `setup()` function of {file}`conf.py`):

```
from sphinx.ext.autodoc import cut_lines
app.connect('autodoc-process-docstring', cut_lines(4, what=['module']))
```

This can (and should) be used in place of `automodule_skip_lines`.
Some useful event listener factories for autodoc-process-docstring.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.between
documentation:
Return a listener that either keeps, or if *exclude* is True excludes,
lines between lines that match the *marker* regular expression.  If no line
matches, the resulting docstring would be empty, so no change will be made
unless *keepempty* is true.

If *what* is a sequence of strings, only docstrings of a type in *what* will
be processed.

functions.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.autodoc_attrgetter
documentation: Alternative getattr() for types

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc._All
documentation: A special value for :\*-members: that matches to any member.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc._Empty
documentation: A special value for :exclude-members: that never matches to any member.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Options
documentation: A dict/attribute hybrid that returns None on nonexisting keys.
This class is used only in ``sphinx.ext.autodoc.directive``,
But we define this class here to keep compatibility (see #4538)

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.ObjectMember
documentation:
A member of object.

This is used for the result of `Documenter.get_module_members()` to
represent each member of the object.

:::{Note}
An instance of this class behaves as a tuple of (name, object)
for compatibility to old Sphinx. The behavior will be dropped
in the future. Therefore extensions should not use the tuple
interface.
:::

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.get_attr
documentation: getattr() override for types such as Zope interfaces.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.can_document_member
documentation: Called to see if a member can be documented by this Documenter.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.documenters
documentation: Returns registered Documenter classes

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.add_line
documentation: Append one line of generated reST to the output.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.resolve_name
documentation:
Resolve the module and name of the object to document given by the
arguments and the current module/class.

Must return a pair of the module name and a chain of attributes; for
example, it would return `('zipfile', ['ZipFile', 'open'])` for the
`zipfile.ZipFile.open` method.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.parse_name
documentation:
Determine what module to import and what attribute to document.

Returns True and sets *self.modname*, *self.objpath*, *self.fullname*,
*self.args* and *self.retann* if parsing and resolving was successful.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.import_object
documentation:
Import the object given by *self.modname* and *self.objpath* and set
it as *self.object*.

Returns True if successful, False if an error occurred.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.get_real_modname
documentation:
Get the real module name of an object to document.

It can differ from the name of the module through which the object was
imported.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.check_module
documentation:
Check if *self.object* is really defined in the module given by
*self.modname*.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.format_args
documentation:
Format the argument signature of *self.object*.

Should return None if the object does not have a signature.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.format_name
documentation:
Format the name of *self.object*.

This normally should be something that can be parsed by the generated
directive, but doesn't need to be (Sphinx will display it unparsed
then).

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.format_signature
documentation:
Format the signature (arguments and return annotation) of the object.

Let the user process it via the `autodoc-process-signature` event.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.add_directive_header
documentation: Add the directive header and options to the generated content.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.get_doc
documentation:
Decode and return lines of the docstring(s) for the object.

When it returns None, autodoc-process-docstring will not be called for this
object.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.process_doc
documentation: Let the user process the docstrings before adding them.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.add_content
documentation: Add content from docstrings, attribute documentation and user.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.get_object_members
documentation:
Return `(members_check_module, members)` where `members` is a
list of `(membername, member)` pairs of the members of *self.object*.

If *want_all* is True, return all members.  Else, only return those
members given by *self.options.members* (which may also be None).

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.filter_members
documentation:
Filter the given member list.

Members are skipped if

- they are private (except if given explicitly or the private-members
  option is set)
- they are special methods (except if given explicitly or the
  special-members option is set)
- they are undocumented (except if the undoc-members option is set)

The user can override the skipping decision by connecting to the
`autodoc-skip-member` event.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.document_members
documentation:
Generate reST for member documentation.

If *all_members* is True, document all members, else those given by
*self.options.members*.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.sort_members
documentation: Sort the given member list.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.Documenter.generate
documentation:
Generate reST for the object given by *self.name*, and possibly for
its members.

If *more_content* is given, include that content. If *real_modname* is
given, use that module name to find attribute docs. If *check_module* is
True, only generate if the object is defined in the module name it is
imported from. If *all_members* is True, document all members.

A Documenter knows how to autodocument a single object type.  When
registered with the AutoDirective, it will be used to document objects
of that type when needed by autodoc.

Its *objtype* attribute selects what auto directive it is assigned to
(the directive name is 'auto' + objtype), and what directive it generates
by default, though that can be overridden by an attribute called
*directivetype*.

A Documenter has an *option_spec* that works like a docutils directive's;
in fact, it will be used to parse an auto directive's options that matches
the Documenter.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.ModuleDocumenter

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.ModuleDocumenter.get_module_members

Mixin for DataDocumenter and AttributeDocumenter to provide the feature for

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.UninitializedGlobalVariableMixin

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.DataDocumenter.update_annotations
documentation: Update __annotations__ to support type_comment and so on.
Specialized Documenter subclass for data items.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.MethodDocumenter

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.MethodDocumenter.merge_default_value
documentation: Merge default values of actual implementation to the overload variants.
Issue #34730: The format_signature() of the class MethodDocumenter
supports overloaded methods via inspect.signature(), which does not work
with Sage yet. Hence the method was removed from here.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.MethodDocumenter.annotate_to_first_argument
documentation: Annotate type hint to the first argument of function if needed.

class_variables.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.MethodDocumenter.priority
documentation: must be more than FunctionDocumenter
Specialized Documenter subclass for methods (normal, static and class).

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.NonDataDescriptorMixin
documentation:
Mixin for AttributeDocumenter to provide the feature for supporting non
data-descriptors.

:::{note}
This mix-in must be inherited after other mix-ins. Otherwise, docstring
and :value: header will be suppressed unexpectedly.
:::

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.SlotsMixin

documentation: Check the subject is an attribute defined in __init__() without comment.

Check the existence of runtime instance attribute after failing to import the
attribute.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.UninitializedInstanceAttributeMixin

documentation: Check the subject is an annotation only attribute.

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.UninitializedInstanceAttributeMixin.import_object
documentation:
Check the exisitence of uninitialized instance attribute when failed to import
the attribute.

Mixin for AttributeDocumenter to provide the feature for supporting uninitialized
instance attributes (PEP-526 styled, annotation only attributes).

Example:

    class Foo:
        attr: int  #: This is a target of this mix-in.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.AttributeDocumenter

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.AttributeDocumenter.update_annotations
documentation: Update __annotations__ to support type_comment and so on.

classes.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.PropertyDocumenter

methods.name: sage_docbuild.ext.sage_autodoc.PropertyDocumenter.import_object
documentation:
Check the exisitence of uninitialized instance attribute when failed to import
the attribute.
Specialized Documenter subclass for properties.
